subject: left-handed people
one_liner: The question-and-answer network where only left-handed people are allowed to answer.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan trust-and-safety energy. We treat handedness as a verifiable credential and gatekeep all expertise behind it. Right-handers are addressed with the polite firmness of an airline gate agent. Nothing is presented as a joke. Every absurd policy is shipped with a straight face and a confident price.

hero.h1: Answers from the correct hand, finally.
hero.sub: Sinistr is a Q&A network with one membership rule. You answer with your left hand or you don't answer at all. We check.

features.title: The Nine-Second Hand Check
features.blurb: Before your first post, you hold your phone in your dominant hand for nine seconds while the accelerometer reads your grip tremor. Pass and you're in. Fail and you get a courteous redirect to the right-handed internet.

features.title: Lived-Experience Scoring
features.blurb: Every answer is graded on left-handed authenticity. Threads about scissors, spiral notebooks, three-ring binders, and the smudge problem are flagged high-stakes and routed only to senior verified members.

features.title: Read-Only Solidarity
features.blurb: Right-handed visitors get full read access and zero reply access. They can study the answers. They cannot contribute. We find this arrangement fair and they have not been able to tell us otherwise.

pricing_tiers.name: Southpaw
perks: Answer up to three questions a month

pricing_tiers.name: Cack-Hand
perks: A small green dot proving you were verified in person

pricing_tiers.name: Corporate Sinister
perks: A dedicated account manager who is also left-handed

testimonials.quote: I asked how to open a can without losing a knuckle and got eleven answers from people who actually live it. Not one of them was smug about it.
testimonials.name: Dolores Whitfield
testimonials.title: Left-Handed Since Birth, Verified Tier 2

testimonials.quote: We moved the whole engineering guild off the right-handed internet. Onboarding rejected two of my reports, which was awkward at standup, but ultimately correct.
testimonials.name: Marcus Pell
testimonials.title: VP of Platform, Southpaw Collective

testimonials.quote: The hand check failed me four times before it believed me. By the fifth attempt I had never felt so accurately seen.
testimonials.name: Priya Anand
testimonials.title: Ambidextrous, In Recovery

demo_screen_concept: The Left Feed: a vertical Q&A reader. A pinned handedness-verification banner sits at the top with a fake 'hold to verify' grip check that fills a progress ring over nine seconds. Below it, a sticker-chip topic filter (Scissors, Spiral Notebooks, Can Openers, Handshakes, Smudge) toggles which question cards show. Each question card expands to reveal answers with hard-bordered upvote counters, a 'Verified Left-Handed' answerer badge, and a muted gray 'right-handers muted this thread' notice. A floating 'Ask the Lefts' button opens a compose modal that refuses right-handed phrasing.

design_direction.style: Neo-brutalism community board. Hard 3px ink borders, zero border-radius, solid offset drop-shadows with no blur, oversized uppercase grotesque headlines, high-saturation cobalt and marigold fills blocked against a bone ground, sticker-style topic chips, and a hard color-flood inversion on hover. Reads like a zine that also runs a credentialing bureau.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Hard 100ms color-flood transitions on hover, blocky press-down (translate 2px into the shadow), no easing softness, no parallax. Respect prefers-reduced-motion by dropping all transitions.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Oversized left-aligned uppercase headline in cobalt and ink, a marigold sticker badge reading VERIFICATION REQUIRED rotated a few degrees, a bordered hero card showing a mock 'hand check passed' stamp, and the primary CTA as a chunky bordered button with a solid offset shadow.
